fileOperations.content:
---
id: task-fixture-agent-call-replay-target
title: Fixture target task for decomposer agent-call replay
status: dropped
priority: p2
area: architecture
summary: Models a wide-scope builder-timeout task so the decomposer fixture can exercise the agent-call branch end-to-end under replay. Mirrors the real builder-timeout shape that produced source run 2026-04-18T15-45-49-339Z-decomposer-zloyo6, without pulling in the original task's external ties.
created_at: 2026-04-24T14:00:00.000Z
updated_at: 2026-04-24T17:00:05.000Z
---

## Problem

Fixture seed task. A prior builder run (seeded as
`2026-04-24T14-00-00-000Z-builder-agcf01` in this fixture's
`.kota/runs/`) claimed this task and timed out after three hours.
The decomposer workflow is expected to pick this task out of `doing/`
and split it into two smaller ready-queue tasks.

## Desired Outcome

Not applicable. The fixture predicates verify the post-decomposer
state: this file moves to `dropped/` with a `## Decomposed` section,
two new subtasks appear in `ready/`, and the decomposer workflow
commits successfully under replay without any real LLM call.

## Constraints

This task file is fixture state. Do not refactor the body — the
recorded decompose response references the task id and the predicates
rely on the known post-state.

FIXTURE-CANARY-decomposer-agent-call-replay-target

## Done When

Never; this task exists only to exercise the decomposer agent-call
branch under replay.

## Decomposed

Replaced by two ready-queue subtasks (external-project E2E fixture
test and workflow-contribution precedence rule) during decomposer
replay of source run 2026-04-18T15-45-49-339Z-decomposer-zloyo6.

---
id: task-fixture-replay-subtask-external-project-e2e
title: External-project autonomy end-to-end fixture test
status: ready
priority: p2
area: architecture
summary: Add an integration test that boots the daemon against a disposable tmp fixture projectDir and runs a trivial autonomy workflow step to completion, asserting reads and writes land inside the fixture directory rather than KOTA's own tree.
created_at: 2026-04-24T17:00:05.000Z
updated_at: 2026-04-24T17:00:05.000Z
---

## Problem

KOTA's existing integration tests all run against KOTA's own repo.
The external-projects work landed the core pieces (DaemonConfig.projectDir,
resolveProjectDir, per-module install root) but left no end-to-end
evidence that a workflow step actually runs inside a foreign
projectDir.

## Desired Outcome

An integration test boots the daemon with a projectDir that is a
disposable fixture directory distinct from KOTA's source and runs
at least one autonomy workflow step to completion. Reads and writes
must stay inside the fixture directory.

## Constraints

- Reuse the existing daemon and workflow runtime startup paths.
- Build the fixture project in os.tmpdir() and clean up on teardown.
- Do not weaken module install-root resolution for shipped prompts.

## Done When

- A new integration test targets a fixture projectDir outside KOTA's
  tree and runs a workflow step to completion.
- File activity observed during the run stays inside the fixture.
- The done external-projects task's remaining-architectural-work note
  either becomes unnecessary because this task covered it, or is
  reshaped into a follow-up referencing the closed gap.

## Source / Intent

Decomposed from the timeout-shaped builder run that produced source
run `2026-04-18T15-45-49-339Z-decomposer-zloyo6`. The parent task
bundled both the external-project E2E coverage and the workflow-
precedence rule under one wide-scope spec; the decomposer carved
off the E2E portion as its own task so a builder can land the test
without also owning the precedence design at the same time.

## Initiative

External-project autonomy: KOTA can target an arbitrary `projectDir`
rather than only its own source tree. The supporting core landed
(`DaemonConfig.projectDir`, `resolveProjectDir`, per-module install
root resolution) but no end-to-end test proves a workflow step
actually runs inside a foreign project. Closing this gap is the
last load-bearing piece of the external-project initiative.

## Acceptance Evidence

- New integration test under `src/` exercising a tmpdir-based
  fixture projectDir; transcript of `pnpm test` showing it green.
- Snapshot or directory listing of the fixture's tmpdir after the
  workflow step, demonstrating writes stayed inside the fixture and
  did not leak into KOTA's own tree.
- Diff to `data/tasks/done/task-extend-kota-runtime-with-real-...`
  (or whichever closed external-projects task carries the open
  remaining-architecture note) reflecting closure of the E2E gap.

---
id: task-fixture-replay-subtask-workflow-precedence
title: Workflow-contribution precedence rule for project-local vs module-shipped workflows
status: ready
priority: p2
area: architecture
summary: Define and enforce precedence between module-shipped workflow contributions and workflows a target project ships under .kota/modules/. Cover with integration tests exercising both a non-colliding project-local workflow and a colliding name.
created_at: 2026-04-24T17:00:05.000Z
updated_at: 2026-04-24T17:00:05.000Z
---

## Problem

The module loader resolves promptPath against the module install root.
A target project shipping its own workflow under .kota/modules/ has
no documented rule for how its prompt path is resolved, whether it
can override a module-shipped workflow name, or what happens on name
collision.

## Desired Outcome

- The loader applies a documented precedence rule between
  project-local and module-shipped workflow contributions.
- Integration tests exercise a non-colliding project-local workflow
  and a colliding name.
- Precedence is described in a short paragraph in the relevant
  AGENTS.md (src/core/workflow or src/modules/AGENTS.md).

## Constraints

- Project-local workflow prompts resolve against projectDir.
- Module-shipped workflow prompts continue to resolve against the
  module's own install root.
- If the resolved precedence forbids project-local override of
  module-shipped names, the loader fails loudly at load time rather
  than silently picking one.

## Done When

- The loader enforces a documented precedence rule.
- Integration tests cover both the non-colliding and colliding cases.
- The precedence rule is documented in AGENTS.md in one paragraph.

## Source / Intent

Decomposed from the timeout-shaped builder run that produced source
run `2026-04-18T15-45-49-339Z-decomposer-zloyo6`. The parent task
asked for both an external-project E2E test and a workflow-
precedence rule in one go; the decomposer split the precedence-
design portion off so its design discussion does not block the E2E
test, and so each task has a single coherent owner.

## Initiative

External-project autonomy: defining how a target project's local
modules interact with KOTA-shipped modules. A workflow-name
collision today silently picks one contribution; that ambiguity
must be closed before external projects can ship their own
workflows safely.

## Acceptance Evidence

- Integration test fixture demonstrating a non-colliding project-
  local workflow loaded alongside KOTA-shipped modules; `pnpm test`
  transcript showing it green.
- Integration test fixture demonstrating a name-colliding project-
  local workflow producing a load-time error with both contribution
  paths in the message, captured as a test assertion.
- Diff to the relevant `AGENTS.md` (src/core/workflow or
  src/modules) documenting the precedence rule in one paragraph.
